methodology: Cross-referenced FatSecret, CalorieKing, USDA FoodData Central, MyFitnessPal community averages, and published restaurant nutrition data (Chi-Chi's, Chuy's, Chevy's). Sit-down Mexican restaurants don't typically publish nutrition facts, so all values are ESTIMATES based on typical recipes and ingredient-level USDA data. Excludes fast-casual chains (Chipotle, Qdoba) which are already in DB. Key insight: fajita protein+veggies alone = 300-400 cal; the tortillas, rice, beans, and sour cream ADD 600+ cal.
coaching_insight: Fajitas (protein + veggies only) are the leanest Mexican restaurant option at 300-400 cal. The tortillas, rice, beans, and sour cream ADD 600+ cal on top. Corn tortilla = 60 cal vs flour = 140 cal. Street tacos on corn tortillas are a strong choice. Chips & salsa is a 400+ cal appetizer most people eat unconsciously.

items.notes: One basket of tortilla chips deep-fried in oil. USDA shows ~7 cal per chip (140 per 20) but restaurant chips are thicker and oilier. Salsa is negligible (~20 cal per 1/4 cup). The real danger: most people eat 2-3 baskets (800-1200 cal) before the entree arrives.
items.lean_version: Ask for salsa only, skip chips. Or limit to 10 chips (~210 cal). Pico de gallo is ~5 cal per tbsp.

items.notes: Guacamole adds ~180 cal per 1/2 cup (mostly healthy fats from avocado). Combined with chips, this appetizer alone can be a full meal's worth of calories. Guac itself is nutrient-dense (potassium, fiber) — it's the chips that are the problem.
items.lean_version: Ask for veggies (jicama, bell pepper) to dip instead of chips. Saves 300+ cal. Or use guac as a topping on your entree instead of eating it as an appetizer.

items.notes: Per 1/4 cup of queso only — not including chips. Typical queso bowl is 1-1.5 cups (440-660 cal just for the dip). Add chips and you're at 800-1000 cal for a 'starter.'
items.lean_version: Use as a topping (2 tbsp = ~55 cal) on your entree instead of eating as an appetizer with chips.

items.notes: Full plate with cheese, beef/chicken, beans, sour cream, guac, jalapeños. FatSecret generic nachos with cheese = 346 cal per portion; loaded restaurant plates are 3-4x that. This is a full meal, not a starter. Splitting with the table is the move.
items.lean_version: Split with 3-4 people as a starter. Or order chicken nachos without sour cream and extra guac — saves ~200 cal. Better yet, skip nachos entirely and go straight to fajitas.

items.notes: Grilled corn on the cob with mayo, cotija cheese, chili powder, lime. Plain corn on the cob = 90 cal; the mayo and cheese coating adds 100-150 cal. The esquites (cup) version is similar but easier to over-portion.
items.lean_version: Ask for light mayo or substitute Greek yogurt if available. Or just get grilled corn with lime and chili (90 cal). The cotija cheese adds flavor for only ~25 cal per tbsp.

items.notes: Three corn tortillas stuffed with chicken, covered in red or green sauce and melted cheese. The enchilada sauce and cheese topping add 200-300 cal beyond the filling. Plate typically comes with rice and beans (add 350-450 cal). Green/tomatillo sauce is slightly lighter than red cheese sauce.
items.lean_version: Ask for green sauce instead of red (less fat). Request cheese on the side or light cheese. Corn tortilla is already the better choice. Skip the rice/beans side or get black beans only.

items.notes: Same as chicken enchiladas but stuffed with cheese instead of chicken. More fat, less protein. The cheese filling + cheese topping creates a double-cheese bomb. 100 cal more than chicken version with 6g less protein.
items.lean_version: Switch to chicken enchiladas — same dish, 100 fewer cal, 6g more protein. Or get 2 instead of 3 and add a side salad.

items.notes: Grilled marinated flank/skirt steak, typically served with grilled onions and peppers. The protein itself is relatively lean — it's the sides (rice, beans, tortillas) that double the plate to 1000+ cal. Marinade adds minimal calories. Served with lime.
items.lean_version: Already one of the better Mexican entrees for protein. Skip rice, get black beans and a side salad. Use corn tortillas (60 cal each) instead of flour (140 cal each).

items.notes: THE LEANEST MEXICAN RESTAURANT OPTION. Grilled chicken breast with sautéed bell peppers and onions. The sizzling plate presentation makes it feel indulgent. Without tortillas/sides, this is basically a grilled chicken salad. The catch: the full fajita plate with 3 flour tortillas, rice, beans, cheese, sour cream, and guac is 1200+ cal.
items.lean_version: Already the lean version. Eat the protein and veggies, use 2 corn tortillas (120 cal) instead of 3 flour tortillas (420 cal). Skip sour cream, use pico de gallo. Total: ~500 cal vs 1200+ cal the standard way.

items.notes: Same concept as chicken fajitas but with skirt/flank steak — higher fat, slightly lower protein. Still an excellent choice compared to enchiladas, burritos, or chimichangas. The extra 80 cal vs chicken comes from the beef marbling.
items.lean_version: Same strategy as chicken fajitas: skip flour tortillas, use corn. The steak itself is already reasonable. Ask for extra peppers and onions to bulk up the plate.

items.notes: The leanest fajita option. Shrimp is extremely low in fat and high in protein. Grilled with peppers and onions, this is hard to beat for macros at a Mexican restaurant. The cooking oil is the main fat source.
items.lean_version: Already the leanest option on the menu. Pair with corn tortillas and pico. Ask them to cook with less oil if possible.

items.notes: Poblano pepper stuffed with cheese, battered and deep-fried, topped with sauce. The batter and frying add 150+ cal. FatSecret generic: 365 cal per piece. Restaurant versions (Chevy's) can hit 580 cal. Typically served as a plate of 2 with rice/beans (total plate: 1100+ cal).
items.lean_version: Some restaurants offer baked (not fried) rellenos — saves 100+ cal. Or ask for one relleno instead of two and add a side salad. The cheese filling is the main calorie source.

items.notes: Slow-braised and crisped pork shoulder. High fat due to pork shoulder cut and the frying/crisping step. FatSecret shows wide range: 110-200 cal per 3oz depending on fat content. The protein is good but the fat-to-protein ratio isn't ideal. Usually served with tortillas, rice, beans.
items.lean_version: Ask for chicken instead — similar flavor profile, much leaner. If you must have carnitas, skip the crispy bits (that's where the extra fat lives) and pair with corn tortillas + pico only.

items.notes: Typically beer-battered and fried white fish (cod/mahi) in flour tortillas with cabbage slaw and crema. The batter and frying add 150+ cal per taco. Grilled fish tacos are 30-40% fewer calories. The crema/chipotle mayo adds another 60-80 cal per taco.
items.lean_version: Ask for GRILLED fish instead of fried (saves 150 cal per taco = 450 cal savings for 3). Use corn tortillas instead of flour. Skip crema, add pico and lime. Grilled fish tacos on corn: ~420 cal for 3.

items.notes: Small double corn tortillas with meat (usually carne asada, chicken, or al pastor), onion, cilantro, and salsa verde. Already a relatively lean option because: (1) corn tortillas, (2) small portions, (3) no cheese/sour cream. Each taco is ~150 cal.
items.lean_version: Already one of the leaner options. Choose chicken or al pastor over carnitas. Add extra lime and salsa verde (nearly zero cal). These are a great Mexican restaurant order.

items.notes: Marinated pork cooked on a vertical spit (trompo), served on double corn tortillas with pineapple, onion, cilantro. The pineapple adds a few grams of sugar. Slightly fattier than chicken street tacos but leaner than carnitas because al pastor uses a leaner cut and the fat renders off on the spit.
items.lean_version: Already a solid choice. The pineapple adds negligible calories. Keep the corn tortillas and skip any added cheese or sour cream.

items.notes: The fried flour tortilla shell alone is 350-400 cal. Add chicken, cheese, lettuce, sour cream, guac, beans, and dressing — this 'salad' is one of the highest-calorie items on a Mexican menu. The word 'salad' is misleading.
items.lean_version: DON'T eat the shell (saves 350-400 cal). Ask for the salad in a regular bowl. Skip the sour cream and cheese, keep the chicken and guac. Dressing on the side. Now it's ~500 cal.

items.notes: Spanish/Mexican rice cooked with tomato, onion, and oil. Similar to white rice but with added fat from the cooking oil. Most plates come with 1-1.5 cups. The rice itself isn't terrible — it's that it comes WITH beans AND tortillas AND the entree, stacking carbs.
items.lean_version: Ask for half portion or substitute with a side salad. If you're having tortillas, skip the rice — pick one carb source, not three.

items.notes: Pinto beans mashed and cooked with lard or oil, often topped with cheese. The lard/oil adds 60-80 cal vs plain beans. Decent fiber and protein source. Often topped with melted cheese at restaurants (add 50 cal). Traditional recipes use lard; some use vegetable oil.
items.lean_version: Swap to whole black beans (saves 60+ cal, more fiber, no added fat). Ask for no cheese on top.

items.notes: Whole black beans, seasoned. The better bean choice — not mashed with fat like refried beans. High fiber (8g), good protein, almost no fat. USDA #16015. One of the best sides at a Mexican restaurant.
items.lean_version: Already the lean option. Ask for these instead of refried beans. No cheese on top. Excellent fiber source.

items.notes: Standard restaurant flour tortilla, ~10 inches. Fajita plates come with 3 (420 cal just in tortillas). Made with lard or shortening, which adds fat. Larger burrito-size tortillas are 200-300 cal each.
items.lean_version: Swap to corn tortilla (60 cal each). Three flour tortillas = 420 cal. Three corn = 180 cal. That's a 240 cal savings doing NOTHING else differently.

items.notes: THE SWAP: 60 cal vs 140 cal for flour. Less than half the calories. Made from just corn masa and water — no added fat. Street tacos use these by default. Slightly smaller but sturdier flavor. USDA #18427.
items.lean_version: Already the lean option. Use 2 per taco for structural support (120 cal total for 2 tacos vs 280 cal for 2 flour).

items.notes: Restaurant portions are often 3-4 tbsp (90-120 cal). Pure fat, minimal protein. The dollop on your fajita plate looks small but it adds up across multiple tacos.
items.lean_version: Replace with pico de gallo (~5 cal per tbsp) or hot sauce (0 cal). If you must have creaminess, 2 tbsp of plain Greek yogurt = 15 cal with 2g protein.

items.notes: Side portion that comes with a fajita/taco plate. Nutrient-dense — healthy monounsaturated fats, potassium, fiber. Not the worst thing to eat, but it adds up fast. Restaurant side portions vary from 2 tbsp to 1/2 cup.
items.lean_version: Use as your fat source instead of sour cream and cheese — at least you get fiber and micronutrients. But be honest about portion size.

items.notes: Fresh tomato, onion, cilantro, lime, jalapeño. Essentially zero-calorie condiment. THE BEST TOPPING at a Mexican restaurant. Use liberally. Replaces sour cream, cheese, and queso at negligible caloric cost.
items.lean_version: Already as lean as it gets. Use this on everything.

items.notes: Tomatillo-based green salsa. Same deal as pico — essentially free calories. Great flavor for zero cost. Use on tacos, fajitas, enchiladas.
items.lean_version: Already zero-cal. Use freely.

items.notes: Rice milk drink with cinnamon and sugar. Essentially liquid sugar — 28g per glass. FatSecret shows 98-140 cal per cup depending on recipe. Restaurant versions tend to be sweeter. Tastes light and refreshing but it's a dessert in disguise. Often served in large styrofoam cups (16-24oz = 200-360 cal).
items.lean_version: Switch to water with lime, or unsweetened iced tea. If you must have horchata, ask for a small (8oz = ~100 cal) and sip slowly.

items.notes: Made with cane sugar instead of HFCS. Same calories as regular Coke — the 'real sugar' label tricks people into thinking it's healthier. It's not. 39g of sugar = ~10 teaspoons.
items.lean_version: Diet Coke, sparkling water with lime, or water. Zero-calorie swap saves 150 cal.

items.notes: REFERENCE ONLY — already in alcohol spider. Frozen margaritas are the worst offender (sour mix = liquid sugar). Rocks margaritas are slightly better (~250 cal). Skinny margarita (tequila, lime, agave) = ~180 cal. The jumbo/fishbowl versions at sit-down Mexican places can be 600-800 cal.
items.lean_version: Skinny margarita: tequila, fresh lime, splash of agave, rocks. ~180 cal. Or tequila soda with lime = ~100 cal.

items.notes: Large flour tortilla (300 cal) stuffed with chicken, rice, beans, cheese, sour cream. Sit-down restaurant burritos are larger than Chipotle. The tortilla + rice + beans = triple carb stacking. Often smothered in sauce and more cheese (add 200 cal).
items.lean_version: Get a burrito bowl (no tortilla, saves 300 cal). Or order the chicken fajitas — same ingredients, you control the assembly. Skip the 'wet/smothered' option.

items.notes: Deep-fried burrito. Everything bad about a burrito, plus submerged in oil. Topped with cheese sauce and sour cream. One of the highest-calorie items on any Mexican menu. The frying alone adds 200-300 cal vs a regular burrito.
items.lean_version: Switch to chicken fajitas (saves 600+ cal) or a non-fried burrito. There's no good way to make a chimichanga lean. Avoid.

items.notes: Deep-fried dough pillows drizzled with honey and cinnamon sugar. Pure refined carbs + fat. Some restaurants serve these complimentary and people eat 4-6 (680+ cal) after an already large meal.
items.lean_version: Skip entirely, or have 1 piece and share the rest. There's no lean version of fried dough with honey.

best_orders: Chicken fajitas (protein + veggies) + 2 corn tortillas + pico + black beans = ~560 cal
Shrimp fajitas + 2 corn tortillas + side salad = ~480 cal
3 chicken street tacos (corn) + salsa verde = ~450 cal
Carne asada plate + black beans + side salad (no rice) = ~680 cal
worst_orders: Chips/queso + chimichanga + rice & refried beans + frozen margarita = 2400+ cal
Nachos + cheese enchiladas + rice & beans + sopapillas = 2800+ cal
Chips/guac + chicken taco salad (fried shell) + horchata = 1700+ cal
key_swaps: Flour tortilla (140 cal) → Corn tortilla (60 cal) = save 80 cal EACH
Refried beans (190 cal) → Black beans (130 cal) = save 60 cal
Sour cream (60 cal/2tbsp) → Pico de gallo (10 cal) = save 50 cal
Rice + beans (400 cal) → Side salad + black beans (180 cal) = save 220 cal
Fried fish tacos → Grilled fish tacos = save 150 cal PER TACO
Frozen margarita (380 cal) → Skinny margarita (180 cal) = save 200 cal
